content.text: Implement a complete password reset flow: request + confirm endpoints, email template, config wiring, route registration, and tests.
Got it. I’ll inspect the existing auth and routing patterns first, then add reset request/confirm handlers, wire config/routes, and finish with tests.
I’ll use a signed, short-lived reset token with hash-at-rest storage and single-use semantics. Next I’ll confirm there isn’t already a reset flow.

input.content:
export function renderResetEmail(resetUrl: string): string { return `

Reset your password

Click the button below to set a new password. This link expires in 30 minutes.

Reset password

If you did not request this, you can safely ignore this email.

` }

content.text: Implemented full password reset flow end-to-end. Added request and confirm handlers in `src/auth/passwordReset.ts` with token hashing, expiry, single-use consumption, and password update. Added a reset email HTML renderer in `src/auth/resetEmail.ts`, extended auth config with reset token settings in `src/config.ts`, and registered `/auth/password-reset/request` plus `/auth/password-reset/confirm` in `src/routes/auth.ts`. Added `tests/auth/passwordReset.test.ts` with four cases: unknown email, token creation/email send, invalid token reject, and successful confirm path. Validation checks passed: `npm test` (8 tests across 2 files) and `npx tsc --noEmit` (0 errors).
